post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 182: Newbie Setup Mafia!
Credits to fdas for the post template
Story
Today is the yearly AoPS Mafia celebration. This year, everyone agreed to bring a bunch of new players in order to attract more people than ever. However, the original Mafia setup was found dead. No one really cared for it, but everyone was scared about what the killers would do next. There were some incredibly insane setups that were obviously not invited, but they couldn't be this powerful, right?

Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • 1.5: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.6: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.7: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.8: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.19: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm by posting Confirm
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point after Day 1 in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point after Day 1 in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.10: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.11: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
    • 2.12: This game uses deadline hang, in which, once deadline passes, the person with the most votes is hung
    • 2.13: In the case of a tie, the person to b hung will be chosen randomly.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, then the person with the highest number of votes will be hung(see above)
    • 3.5: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.6: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.7: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.8: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.11: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.12: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.13: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, SE’s up to one, and IC’s none. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (sudzoof), and Thornclaw9000 are the moderator and co-moderator of Mafia Game 182, respectively
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
  • The setup is 2d3.
  • In total, there are 9 possible setups:
    • A1: Mafia Goon, Mafia Roleblocker vs. Town Cop, Town Neapolitan, 5x Town Vanilla
    • A2: Mafia Goon, Mafia Roleblocker vs. Town Jailkeeper, Town Doctor, 5x Town Vanilla
    • A3: Mafia Goon, Mafia Roleblocker vs. Town Doctor, Town Cop, 5x Town Vanilla
    • B1: Mafia Goon, Mafia Rolecop vs. Town Cop, Town Tracker, 5x Town Vanilla
    • B2: Mafia Goon, Mafia Rolecop vs. Town Jailkeeper, Town Tracker, 5x Town Vanilla
    • B3: Mafia Goon, Mafia Rolecop vs. Town Doctor, Town Neapolitan, 5x Town Vanilla
    • C1: Mafia Goon, Mafia Goon vs. Town Cop, 6x Town Vanilla
    • C2: Mafia Goon, Mafia Goon vs. Town Jailkeeper, 6x Town Vanilla
    • C3: Mafia Goon, Mafia Goon vs. Town Doctor, Town Tracker, 5x Town Vanilla
  • The setup chosen will be randomly chosen
  • Mafia have daychat. This means that the mafia members can always communicate with each other, even when it is day.
  • The Mafia Roleblocker has priority verses the Town Jailkeeper when they target each other


Role PMs
Vanilla Townie PM wrote:
You are a currently evaluated Newbie Setup(Vanilla Townie). Unlike for the actual newbies, your promotion is actively being discussed about. However, as you have not been verified yet, you don't have any special abilities other than your intelligence and your vote. You win when all the mafia are dead.
Town Cop PM wrote:
You are 2of4(Town Cop) The second oldest of the Mafia setups, you know the behavior patterns of everyone else, allowing you to, once per night, investigate someone, discerning whether or not they have malicious intent. You win when all the mafia are dead.
Town Neapolitan PM wrote:
You are F11(Town Neapolitan). The oldest of the Mafia setups, you mostly know the behavior patterns of everyone else. However, as you have spent way too much time with the mafia after a majority of games were won by them, you can not tell the difference between them and those that have power. Once per night, you can investigate someone, determining whether or not they have power. You win when all the mafia are dead.
Town Jailkeeper wrote:
You are Matrix6(Town Jailkeeper). Being both the most used setup and being the cause of the BP claim strat, you have the most power, being able to dictate the course of others' actions, both offensively and defensively. Once per night, you can visit someone, protecting them from any assassination attempts that night, while preventing then from acting that night as well. You win when all the mafia are dead.
Town Doctor wrote:
You are Backup6(Town Doctor). Despite being prevented from using your bulletproof vest, you still secretly have it. Once per night, you can temporarily give your vest to someone, granting them protection from assassination attempts for the night. You win when all the mafia are dead.
Town Tracker wrote:
You are 2d3(Town Tracker). Despite being very popular elsewhere, you are new to here. However, this means that you can usually go unnoticed. Once per night, you can visit someone, allowing you to discover where they went that night. You win when all the mafia are dead.
Mafia Goon wrote:
You are whatever the heck Game 57's setup is supposed to be(Mafia Goon). You are angry about not even being invited, and want vengeance, along with your partner [name]. Each night, you and your partner can choose to assassinate someone. You win when the mafia outnumber town, or when both sides have equal numbers.
Mafia Roleblocker wrote:
You are whatever the heck Game 53's setup is supposed to be(Mafia Roleblocker). You are angry about not even being invited, and want vengeance, along with your partner [name]. Each night, you and your partner can choose to assassinate someone. You alone, being the first newbie setup to be so absurd, can stun someone, preventing then from taking any actions that night. You win when the mafia outnumber town, or when both sides have equal numbers.


Signups(9)
1. kevinmathz
2. jeffisepic
3. MY-2
4. hdgehrke
5. NDMath
6. Thundius
7. Archimedes15
8. Kbwuf
9. Irshad


Significant Events


Warnings


Do not post until this line is crossed out
You may now post.
Day 1 starts on 2/27 9:30 P.M. ET and ends on 3/13 9.30 P.M. ET.

post_rendered:
Since hdgehrke more or less makes the same group of points, I will reply to his misconceptions, which split into his annoyance at my disapproval of his jeffisepic reaction test, and his annoyance at my lack of "analysis".
1. I am not saying reaction tests are bad. I am saying that using it as a quick gimmick to attempt to get results is spinning wheels in order to get no result, like your "pressure vote". If jeffisepic is not likely to get hung on D1, he is unlikely to submit to your pressure. Preinformation of the gimmicky nature associated with "reaction test" (as compared to the general category of "reaction test") reduces the value, which is different than the wide dialogue I am attempting to create.
2. Useful analysis (as you are likely finding now) is difficult in a post-Irshad point of the game, with a soft majority (I+mathjay are unlikely to vote for anyone else). Since no major mafiaslips have been found, only ones more likely to be bad-town (this post) or inactive-town (MY-2, kevinmathz) slips or troll-town (jeffisepic) slips, it requires almost a restart of day 1 in order to find new conflicts. It would be nice if several could happen, to allow a comparison of magnitude and a lack of awkward gaps. I am trying to find slips, which requires activity and helping the town be productive and ready to find mafia, instead of moping around in inactivity or overfocus on Irshad. I want to get this done early, to allow productivity later. I'm not releasing real readlists yet, since they look like the readlist at the bottom of this post. Most posts are not "HANG THEM NOW" since I want to get people in a position of several mafiesque posts (or a few exceptionally mafiesque posts) if the other players are not as bad as Irshad.
I'm also using language asking questions without explicitly accusing, like the jeffisepic agree to hang him day 2 (implying a lean mafia reading), or the kevinmathz-Irshad commentary (saying the "nobody is disputing" is similar to asking why this was done). The Archimedes15 post is a critique of Irshad, since he is trying to set up a counterwagon with no support whatsoever.
Vague Readlist
mathjay: Likely town, only mafiesque move the likely-joke wagon
Archimedes15: Lean town, only mafiesque move the likely-inept quickhang
hdgehrke: Null, due to inactivity and inept posting (not lean mafia, since it seems to show logic that seems townish)
kevinmathz: Null, due to inactivity
NDMath: Null, due to inactivity
MY-2: Null tilt mafia, mostly due to lurking and newbie-card post
jeffisepic: Null lean mafia, mostly due to random posts
Irshad: Likely mafia, due to literally every post

Sorry, I was at a math meet yesterday.
Post 281 replies (note most points about lack of new points have ending due to kevinmathz and MY-2 new posts)
I'll post in a block, but I'll space it out a bit to ease the reading. I'll skip the critiques assuming that either hdgehrke is right (i.e, "I like how I am annoyed by scumminess") or the critiques of my "large words".

I am using reactions tests in two ways. First, I am using reaction test as the general attempts to post in order to get evidence of scummy activity, and I am also using reaction test as a quick gimmick, usually used as an excuse for bad posting or as a test with a short horizon, and mostly no follow-up. Yours was unlikely (not guaranteed, but that's an unfair standard of evidence) to find real evidence as the warning of a reaction test makes it more likely to be the second (see any mafia game mentioning reaction tests), allowing an easy ignore or a flippant remark to end it (see: the lack of jeffisepic follow-up). "Throwing a curveball" by adding a warning does not necessarily help, as saying "I am a mafioso" does not help as town (or, saying "I am not taking this attempt seriously" does not help as an investigator).

It is good to make people make scumslips, but this is not a way to get good evidence. A "soft majority" is a voting bloc unlikely to be shifted enough to find a hang frontrunner.

My lack of your kind of analysis is due to having a lack of good posts at that time (as is clear by this being the attack you chose). Many different attacks allows a larger sample size, with more scummy behaviors being found as compared with only one person to choose for hanging, limiting choices. I was the first person to try to move from Irshad, despite your allegations otherwise. Talking to people is also the main way to get slips (imagine a mafia game with no posts or back-and-forths), and is certainly "how mafia works".

I slight scumread jeffisepic since he has had prior town behavior like this. "Inept" is easily found with a quick google search. It can in part be defined as your line of attack, which is why I proposed it as an idea. For Irshad, the main things are his selective RVS behavior (as noted by a post you didn't include in your ISO), lack of effort to study stuff that would allow him to be a better town player, and his selective reads ("jeffisepic is a mafioso" "he pardoned me from d1 hang" "I have no reads" his "awesome readlist"). 1234

I'm sorry for typing differently than you. You will likely have to deal with it.
